title: Isocitrate dehydrogenase
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"a16ho","text":"Isocitrate dehydrogenase is an enzyme which converts isocitrate to 2-oxoglutarate in the TCA cycle.  IDH2 uses NADP and IDH3 uses NAD.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"d94gv","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"debm3","text":"Our IDH2/3 assay examines accumulation of NADH/NADPH over time. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"5a257","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ccaip","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"cqc4q","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"image","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"61910EDA341F11EE9D4302C0B41BC903","height":124,"is_video":false,"legend":"{\"blocks\":[{\"key\":\"esbtq\",\"text\":\"Reaction for IDH3, IDH2 uses NADP as its substrate and detected at OD 340 nm as well\",\"type\":\"unstyled\",\"depth\":0,\"inlineStyleRanges\":[],\"entityRanges\":[],\"data\":[]}],\"entityMap\":{}}","mime":"image/png","original_name":"image.png","placeholder":"https://content.protocols.io/public/e855144bdecf75d814931753c11cff3f8c6bead8b0a03fbd2d68f941d9122f3a/b65dbc5gx.png","source":"https://content.protocols.io/public/e855144bdecf75d814931753c11cff3f8c6bead8b0a03fbd2d68f941d9122f3a/b65dbc5gx.png","type":1,"width":437}}}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"bt0qo","text":"Make sure have adequate amount of IDH buffer and that exactly pH 7.4 (best if prepared fresh with each assay)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"576fi","text":"Make sure that adequte mitochondrial lysate","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"6ro5c","text":"Make sure spectrophotometer is set, wells labeled and plate ready to be read immediately","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"dnrj5","text":"0.5 M Potassium Phosphate Buffer pH 7.4 (room temp) ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"a1lt3","text":"1 M MgCl2 (room temp)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sub","offset":8,"length":2}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"abetv","text":"100 mM NADP (-20) (made from β-Nicotinamide Adenine Dinucleotide Phosphate (CAS 53-59-8, kept at -20); dissolved in dW)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"dcrif","text":"1 M Isocitrate (-20) (made from DL-isocitric acid (CAS 1637-73-6, kept at room temp); dissolved in dW)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Prepare the IDH buffer new (Place on ice)

To make 10 mL IDH buffer 10 mM potassium phosphtate pH 7.4 (200 μL 0.5 M potasium phosphate pH 7.4), 2 mM MgCl 2 (20μL 1M MgCl 2 ), 1mM NADP (100 μL 100mM NADP) 5mM isocitrate (50 μL 1M isocitrate) Add water to make 10 mL after pH ing to 7.4
Prepare mitochondrial lysates per protocol (will need 15-30 μg in 20 μL)
Load 20 μL into 96 well plate in triplicate
Set spectrophotometer to Kinetic, dual wavelength 340, 380 nm, 30 reads, interval 30 sec at 30°C
Add 200 μL of IDH buffer to each well of plate (use a multi-chamber pipetter) sitting on the holder for the spectrometer and mix by pipetting in and out 2x and start spectrophotometer
Calculate relative activity from the values of OD 340
creator: Kimberly Chapman
